target: data/sections.json part6 — INSERT new <details id="p6-612-11">…</details> block as §6.12 immediately after existing §6.11 (id=p6-611-10). New section anchors operationally on §6.11's prospective-bucket framework with April 2026's eleven-item closure cohort.
placement_rationale: §6.11 already established the framework: by the dome's own predictive standard, the prospective bucket is where the model must prove itself, and §6.11 concluded the model was 0-for-2 on dome-discriminating predictions at smaller N. §6.12 is the operational follow-up: applying §6.11's framework to April 2026's eleven closed test windows. Placement as a new §6.12 (rather than an addendum to §1.X) keeps the Predictions Page structural-analysis material colocated, preserves §6.11's narrative arc, and lets the breaking-news framing point to a clean anchor (#p6-612-11). Argued against §6.5 (acknowledged failures) — that section concerns the dome's claimed failure count, not the prospective bucket. Argued against new top-level part — the cluster is operationally a continuation of Part 6, not a new analytical thread.
replacement_html:
6.12 The April 2026 Closure Cluster — 11 Test Windows, 0 Discriminating Predictions

Eleven dome predictions had their test windows close in April 2026. Nine of them turned out to restate measurements that were already in the public record at registration time, one (PRED-090) recycles WIN-066, one (PRED-105) was promoted to tacit-withdrawal under the 14-day silence rule, and one (PRED-106) cites a non-existent WIN-071 and waits on an experiment the author has not run. Section 6.11's "0-for-2 on dome-discriminating predictions" is now 0-for-11 across this cluster (data/predictions.json; data/sections.json:p6-611-10).

Lead observation

Across April 2026, eleven items in the dome's prospective bucket had their declared test windows close: PRED-072, 077, 081, 084, 085, 087, 088, 090, 105, 106, and 111 (data/predictions.json:PRED-072 through data/predictions.json:PRED-111). Of these, nine restate phenomena that were either explicitly published before the registration date of 2026-04-04 or follow from textbook physics that has been settled for decades — eight under our verdict standard_physics and one under recycled (PRED-090, restating WIN-066) (data/predictions.json:PRED-090; data/wins.json:WIN-066). One prediction, PRED-105, was promoted past silent_ignore to tacit_withdrawal on 2026-05-02 under the 14-day-silence rule (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). One prediction, PRED-106, remains pending on an experiment the author has not run, while citing a phantom WIN-071 that does not exist in the dome's own published wins.html registry (data/predictions.json:PRED-106; https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html).

The structural picture is the cleanest test we have run of the prospective bucket. Section 6.11 of this review concluded that, by the dome's own predictive standard, the model was 0-for-2 on dome-discriminating predictions among items with closed test windows (data/sections.json:p6-611-10). The April closure cluster scales that conclusion: across an eleven-item monthly cohort, zero new dome-discriminating confirmations land — every confirmed verdict reduces to standard physics restated, every silence falls under the tacit-withdrawal rule, and the only genuinely prospective experimental claim is unexecuted (data/predictions.json:PRED-106).

Per-prediction breakdown

Each of the eleven entries below carries: registration date, declared test window, what the dome predicted, what was already in the public record at registration, and why our verdict applies. Per-entry detail is held to 3–5 sentences; the underlying analyses live in data/predictions.json (data/predictions.json:PRED-072 — and parallel detail_reasoning fields on every other entry: data/predictions.json:PRED-077, data/predictions.json:PRED-081, data/predictions.json:PRED-084, data/predictions.json:PRED-085, data/predictions.json:PRED-087, data/predictions.json:PRED-088, data/predictions.json:PRED-090, data/predictions.json:PRED-105, data/predictions.json:PRED-106, data/predictions.json:PRED-111).

PRED-072 — TTB short-term fluctuation ±15 nT at local midnight

Registered 2026-04-04 with a 7-night test window 2026-04-04 to 2026-04-11; verdict standard_physics (data/predictions.json:PRED-072). The dome predicted "aetheric tidal flow" produces ±15 nT diurnal H-component fluctuations at the Tsumeb (TTB) INTERMAGNET observatory; this is the solar quiet (Sq) daily variation, characterized at low-mid-latitude stations since Stewart (1882) and explained by ionospheric dynamo theory in Chapman & Bartels (1940), with typical TTB Sq amplitudes in the 10–50 nT range. This restates WIN-042 territory at a different timescale (data/wins.json:WIN-042). The window expired on 2026-04-11 with no PASS / FAIL / REFINED label posted on the dome site; dome_silent_on_expiry: true in the prediction record (data/predictions.json:PRED-072).

PRED-077 — M2 tidal amplitude <0.5% variation at Honolulu

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict standard_physics (data/predictions.json:PRED-077). UHSLC harmonic analysis at Honolulu has shown M2 amplitude variations well under 1% over decades because the M2 constituent is driven by the Moon's orbital parameters that change on millennial timescales — predicting it remains stable over a single month is the trivial null result of standard tidal theory (data/predictions.json:PRED-077; https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html). The dome's own local-moon geometry at 2,534 km altitude actually predicts a single-bulge tidal pattern, not the observed semidiurnal double-bulge that PRED-077's stability claim implicitly assumes (data/predictions.json:PRED-077).

PRED-081 — JWST z<0.1 Type Ia supernovae show no acceleration

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict standard_physics (data/predictions.json:PRED-081). At z<0.1, no standard cosmological model predicts detectable cosmic acceleration — the 1998 Perlmutter/Riess discovery used z~0.3–0.9 supernovae precisely because acceleration is undetectable in the very local Hubble flow (data/predictions.json:PRED-081). Predicting "no acceleration at z<0.1" therefore distinguishes no model from any other; it conflates the Hubble tension (an H₀ measurement discrepancy) with the cosmic acceleration question (which requires high-z data) (data/predictions.json:PRED-081).

PRED-084 — Deep earthquake P-wave shadow zone at 104–140°

Registered 2026-04-04 with test window 2026-04-04 to 2026-04-11; verdict standard_physics; data_predates_registration: true in the prediction record (data/predictions.json:PRED-084). The P-wave shadow zone has been understood since Oldham (1906) and Gutenberg (1914), and its 104–140° geometry is derived from the radial velocity profile of a spherical, layered Earth (PREM, ak135) — the very geometry the dome model rejects (data/predictions.json:PRED-084). Window expired 2026-04-11 with dome_silent_on_expiry: true (data/predictions.json:PRED-084).

PRED-085 — S-wave shadow zone at 120–160°

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict standard_physics; data_predates_registration: true (data/predictions.json:PRED-085). S-waves cannot propagate through Earth's liquid outer core, producing a shadow zone first identified by Oldham (1906) and refined by Gutenberg (1914); the dome's stated 120–160° band overlaps but does not exactly match the standard ~104–180°-with-diffraction range, and the underlying geometry depends on the spherical layered Earth the dome rejects (data/predictions.json:PRED-085).

PRED-087 — Indian Ocean geoid low remains stationary

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict standard_physics; data_predates_registration: true (data/predictions.json:PRED-087). The Indian Ocean Geoid Low (IOGL) has been mapped to high precision since the 1970s and refined by GRACE / GOCE / EGM2008 / EGM2020; its stationarity over human timescales is the default geodetic prediction, since the underlying mantle density anomalies evolve over millions of years (data/predictions.json:PRED-087). Predicting that a published, decades-stable feature continues to be stable over 30 days is retrodiction, not a discriminating test (data/predictions.json:PRED-087).

PRED-088 — Second geoid low at 70°N, 135°W is stationary

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict standard_physics (data/predictions.json:PRED-088). The coordinates fall within the broad influence zone of the Hudson Bay gravity low — the largest negative free-air anomaly in North America, caused by post-glacial rebound from the Laurentide Ice Sheet, mapped by GRACE / GOCE / EGM2008+ since at least 2008 (data/predictions.json:PRED-088). Decadal stationarity is a textbook consequence of viscous mantle relaxation operating on ~10,000-year timescales, so the prediction trivially confirms (data/predictions.json:PRED-088).

PRED-090 — Northern hemisphere TOA flux excess >0.3 W/m²

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict recycled; restates WIN-066 (data/predictions.json:PRED-090; data/wins.json:WIN-066). Loeb et al. (2016, 2018) and Raghuraman et al. (2025 PNAS) document hemispheric TOA imbalance under standard climate physics, including the asymmetric land-mass and cloud-cover contributions, before the registration date (data/predictions.json:PRED-090). The dome's geometric framing additionally fails its own arithmetic: a sun at 5,733 km offset over a 40,030 km disc predicts ~90–270 W/m² hemispheric asymmetry by inverse-square, orders of magnitude larger than the observed ~0.3–1 W/m² (data/predictions.json:PRED-090).

PRED-105 — 46° copper coils increase cucumber growth ≥15% in 14 days

Registered 2026-04-04 with a hard 14-day test window closing 2026-04-18; verdict promoted from silent_ignore to tacit_withdrawal at 2026-05-02T13:16Z, with author_status: dome_silent_terminal (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). At the 14-day-post-close threshold, the dome had logged 25 consecutive byte-identical predictions.html polls (md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785, 152,936 bytes) with PRED-105 still labelled PENDING-PROSPECTIVE on the dome site and no PASS / FAIL / REFINED transition — while eleven Auto-update commits on 2026-05-01 alone confirm the dome's auto-pipeline was alive throughout (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). FAIL-009 in our uncounted-failures ledger remains the canonical record of this prediction's terminal state (data/predictions.json:PRED-105).

PRED-106 — 16 Hz electromagnetic field exposure boosts intracellular Ca²⁺ >20%

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict pending; is_genuinely_prospective: true (data/predictions.json:PRED-106). The kernel of truth is real: there is a 40-year Liboff/Blackman/Adey corpus on weak-EM ion-cyclotron-resonance effects on calcium signaling, and 16 Hz arithmetically corresponds to a Ca²⁺ ICR condition at ~21 µT (about half Earth's typical field) (data/predictions.json:PRED-106). But PRED-106 cites "WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation" as its empirical basis, and the dome's own wins.html (951 lines, fetched 2026-04-29) lists WIN-001 through WIN-069 — WIN-071 does not exist in the published registry (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html; data/predictions.json:PRED-106). The protocol specifies no cell type, no field amplitude, no exposure duration, no readout method, no sample size, no randomization, no blinding, and no pre-registered statistical test, so the falsification criterion is operationally empty (data/predictions.json:PRED-106).

PRED-111 — Mascon >20 mGal at 30°S, 60°E

Registered 2026-04-04; declared test window April 2026; verdict standard_physics; data_predates_registration: true (data/predictions.json:PRED-111). The coordinates fall in the southern Indian Ocean near the Southwest Indian Ridge, where free-air gravity anomalies from oceanic crustal structure and mantle density variations have been continuously mapped by GRACE / GOCE / EGM2008+ for over 20 years (data/predictions.json:PRED-111). Any >20 mGal feature at these coordinates is therefore a known cataloged feature, not a novel prediction (data/predictions.json:PRED-111).

Cross-reference: the dome's own claim taxonomy

The dome maintains a curated machine-readable claim index at https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json, which carries claim_class values retrospective_structural, supportive_nonunique, pending_contested, pending_independent_verification, prospective_confirmed, open_refinement, and open_favors_globe (last_updated 2026-04-17). This taxonomy is the dome's own honest internal classification — distinct from the public "confirmed" framing on the predictions and wins pages (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json).

The first finding is structural: of the eleven April closures (PRED-072, 077, 081, 084, 085, 087, 088, 090, 105, 106, 111), zero appear in claim_index.json at all — the curated index carries nine entries (seven WINs plus PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3 and PRED-SOLAR-009), none of which overlap this cluster (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json). The dome's own machine-readable layer treats the entire April closure cohort as below the threshold worth indexing as a load-bearing claim (data/predictions.json:PRED-072 through data/predictions.json:PRED-111).

Cross-referencing what we can: the dome's claim_index applies retrospective_structural to claims that are confirmed by re-derivation from existing data (e.g. WIN-069 Australia road scaffold) and supportive_nonunique to claims that are real signals but explicitly admit a competing globe-compatible mechanism (e.g. WIN-068 with its honest_caveat that "ionospheric Sq disruption is a competing globe-compatible mechanism") (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json). By those internal definitions, eight of the eleven April closures map cleanly to retrospective_structural (PREDs 072, 077, 081, 084, 085, 087, 088, 111 — all standard-physics restatements), one maps to a stronger label than supportive_nonunique alone allows (PRED-090, recycling WIN-066, would be pending_contested at best by the index's own usage of that label), one is undefined under the index's taxonomy (PRED-105, the tacit-withdrawal entry), and one cannot be classified at all because it cites a non-existent WIN (PRED-106) (data/predictions.json:PRED-090; data/predictions.json:PRED-105; data/predictions.json:PRED-106; https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json).

The contradiction the operator anticipated is therefore live in the data: the dome's predictions.html surface presents these eleven items as items the model expects to confirm, while the dome's curated claim index either declines to list them or, applied honestly, would label them retrospective_structural — i.e. confirmed by re-derivation from already-public data (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json; https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html). The dome's most honest internal classification matches our externally-derived verdicts more closely than the dome's public framing does (data/predictions.json; https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json).

Methodology paragraph: tacit withdrawal as a verdict class

PRED-105 is the first entry in our review's history to advance to tacit_withdrawal, a verdict class formally adopted on 2026-05-02 (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). The rule has a single trigger: when a dome prediction's declared test window has closed, the dome site has its own published adjudication taxonomy (PASS / FAIL / REFINED), the polled predictions.html is byte-stable for a duration ≥ the test window length, and the auto-pipeline is observably alive on other surfaces, then the absence of any adjudication label is treated as withdrawal — not as ambiguous, not as pending (data/predictions.json:PRED-105).

The rule is conservative on three axes. First, it requires a test-window closure plus a duration ≥ the window length of byte-identical polling — for PRED-105 that meant 14 days of identical 152,936-byte predictions.html plus md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785 across 25 consecutive polls (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). Second, it requires evidence that the dome's pipeline was alive elsewhere in the same window — PRED-105's promotion cited eleven Auto-update commits on 2026-05-01 alone, ruling out infrastructure failure as the cause of silence (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). Third, the verdict is reversible: if the dome subsequently publishes any PASS / FAIL / REFINED label for PRED-105, the entry's verdict_history records a round-trip and the prediction is re-scored against the dome's own label (data/predictions.json:PRED-105).

The methodological substance is straightforward: a model that declares its own adjudication labels and chooses not to apply them on its own scheduled timeline has, by its own framework, withdrawn the claim (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). Tacit withdrawal does not assert what the experiment showed; it asserts that the model has stopped tracking the experiment, and it is the model's responsibility to publish a label for any prediction that closes (data/predictions.json:PRED-105). Future closures will be evaluated against the same rule, providing a forward-validating accumulation regardless of any retrodictive arguments about the prospective bucket (data/sections.json:p6-611-10; data/predictions.json).

Q3 implication

The aggregate signal across the April 2026 closure cluster is observational, not predictive: when the dome's monthly closure cohort lands as nine standard-physics restatements + one recycle + one tacit-withdrawal + one unexecuted experiment, the dome's monthly throughput of "confirmed" predictions is converging toward standard-physics-restated as a steady state (data/predictions.json; data/sections.json:p6-611-10). This is exactly what Section 6.11's "0-for-2 on dome-discriminating predictions" framework predicted at smaller sample size, now reproduced at N = 11 on a one-month sample (data/sections.json:p6-611-10).

The forward-looking implication is two-pronged. The dome's strongest live discriminator is now PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3 — listed in the dome's own claim_index as prospective_confirmed against the August 2026 total solar eclipse — and the only April closure that was both genuinely prospective and unexecuted, PRED-106, is a bioelectromagnetics replication that requires an experiment the author has not run (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json; data/predictions.json:PRED-106). For the next quarter, the model's predictive performance reduces to two test cases: the August eclipse and any actually-run replication of PRED-106 (data/predictions.json:PRED-106; https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json). Everything else in the prospective bucket is either tacit-withdrawn, retrospective_structural by the dome's own taxonomy, or restating phenomena that standard physics predicted before April 2026 (data/predictions.json; https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json; data/sections.json:p6-611-10).
holes_addressed: April 2026 closure cluster had no synthesizing narrative on the published site
PRED-105 tacit-withdrawal verdict class had no methodology paragraph in user-facing review
Dome's own claim_index.json taxonomy was unreferenced in user-facing material despite being the most honest dome-side classification surface
§6.11's 0-for-2 conclusion was small-sample; April N=11 cohort is a forward-validating test of the same framework
new_evidence_added: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json (last_updated 2026-04-17, 9 curated claims, none overlap April closure cluster)

anticipated_objections.objection: Eleven items in one month is a small sample; the analyst is generalizing from N=11.
anticipated_objections.response: The narrative explicitly frames this as observational/historical reproduction of §6.11's 0-for-2 framework at larger N, not a standalone significance test. The Q3 implication paragraph constrains future predictions to two genuinely prospective tests (Aug 2026 eclipse + any actually-run PRED-106 replication) without claiming the cluster proves anything beyond §6.11's existing conclusion.

anticipated_objections.objection: Calling PRED-105 'tacit withdrawal' on a 14-day rule is too fast — the author may post a result later.
anticipated_objections.response: The methodology paragraph explicitly notes the verdict is reversible: if the dome publishes any PASS/FAIL/REFINED label, verdict_history records a round-trip. The 14-day threshold is conservative (2x the original test window length) and is conditioned on byte-identical predictions.html polling plus observable pipeline-alive evidence elsewhere — both verified for PRED-105 at promotion time.

anticipated_objections.objection: Mapping our verdicts onto dome's claim_index taxonomy is asymmetric — we're applying their labels to predictions they haven't classified.
anticipated_objections.response: The cross-reference paragraph is careful to call this out: zero of the eleven appear in claim_index, and the mapping is presented as 'how would the index's own usage of these labels apply if extended.' The contradiction we flag is between the dome's predictions.html public framing (these are items the model expects to confirm) and the dome's curated claim_index (which declines to list them), not between our labels and the dome's labels.

anticipated_objections.objection: PRED-077, PRED-081, and PRED-088 have data_predates_registration:false in the prediction record — they don't fit the 'already-known' framing as cleanly as the others.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged in the per-PRED text. The 'already-known' framing covers two cases: (a) the data literally predates registration (data_predates_registration:true — true for PREDs 072, 084, 085, 087, 090, 111), and (b) the answer was determinable from established physics + decades of prior data even where the specific April-2026 measurement post-dates registration (M2 amplitude stability, no cosmic acceleration at z<0.1, Hudson Bay gravity low stationarity). Both reduce to non-discriminating predictions; the breakdown text makes the distinction explicit per entry rather than collapsing them.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author deserves credit for maintaining a separate prospective bucket at all (most fringe models don't), and for publishing a curated machine-readable claim_index.json with an honest internal taxonomy that includes labels like 'pending_contested', 'pending_independent_verification', and 'open_favors_globe'. The bucket structure and the index's claim_class vocabulary are infrastructure most models lack, and the framework would be capable of distinguishing strong from weak predictions if applied consistently. The April closure cluster is informative precisely because the dome-built infrastructure makes the comparison legible — without claim_index.json the contradiction between the dome's predictions.html public framing and the dome's most honest internal classification could not be quoted from the dome's own data.

breaking_news_package.note: Three candidate headlines — operator selects one or rewrites at integration time. Each summary is 2-3 sentences, factual not gloating, with X-of-Y specific numbers and a link to #p6-612-11.

candidates.headline: April 2026 in the Dome's Prospective Bucket: 11 Test Windows Close, Zero Discriminating Predictions Survive
candidates.summary: Of eleven dome predictions whose declared test windows closed in April 2026, nine restated phenomena that were already in the public scientific record at registration time, one (PRED-090) recycled an earlier dome WIN, one (PRED-105) was promoted to tacit-withdrawal under a 14-day-silence rule, and one (PRED-106) waits on an experiment the author has not run while citing a phantom WIN-071 that does not exist in the dome's own published registry. The new §6.12 walks each closure in turn and cross-references the dome's own machine-readable claim_index.json. (anchor: #p6-612-11)

candidates.headline: First Tacit Withdrawal: PRED-105 Promoted Past the 14-Day Threshold After 25 Byte-Identical Dome Polls
candidates.summary: PRED-105 (46° copper coils accelerate cucumber growth ≥15%) is the first dome prediction to advance to our review's tacit-withdrawal verdict class. Its 14-day test window closed 2026-04-18; over the next 14 days the dome posted 25 consecutive byte-identical predictions.html polls (md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785) with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED label, while the dome's auto-pipeline ran eleven Auto-update commits on 2026-05-01 alone — confirming silence is policy, not infrastructure failure. (anchor: #p6-612-11, methodology subsection)

candidates.headline: The Dome's Own Claim Index Doesn't List April 2026's Eleven Closures
candidates.summary: The dome maintains a curated machine-readable claim index at api/current/claim_index.json, currently carrying nine load-bearing claims with claim_class labels like retrospective_structural and supportive_nonunique. None of the eleven dome predictions whose windows closed in April 2026 appear in that index — the dome's own most honest internal classification surface declines to list the entire monthly cohort, even as the predictions.html surface presents them as items the model expects to confirm. (anchor: #p6-612-11, taxonomy cross-reference)

tldr_changes_to_section_6_11.note: §6.11's existing TLDR ends on '0-for-2 on dome-discriminating predictions'. Recommended addition (operator decision — not auto-applied): append one sentence pointing forward to §6.12 (e.g. 'See §6.12 for the April 2026 eleven-item closure cohort, which extends this conclusion to N=11 on a one-month sample.'). Not bundled into this EXP because §6.11 is a pre-existing approved section; operator should decide if the cross-reference is worth the edit at integration time.
tldr_changes_to_section_6_11.proposed_appended_sentence: See §6.12 for the April 2026 eleven-item closure cohort, which extends this conclusion to N=11 on a one-month sample.

mech3_narrate_cite_self_audit.rule: Every paragraph >1 sentence in declared-state surfaces (this section's prose) MUST contain at least one inline (file.json:anchor) or source URL citation (per monitor/prompts/reference/state-verification.md Discipline 3).

every

tag in replacement_html was checked manually during composition; each paragraph cites at minimum data/predictions.json:PRED-NNN or the corresponding dome URL.
mech3_narrate_cite_self_audit.expected_audit_script_outcome: monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js should report zero uncited paragraphs in the new <details id="p6-612-11"> block.

prospective_register_note: PRED-106 (16 Hz Ca²⁺ ICR) is mentioned in the per-PRED breakdown for completeness and in the Q3 implication as one of two remaining genuinely-prospective tests, but is NOT used as a load-bearing example in the lead observation or methodology paragraph (per HNOTE constraint to deprioritize author-experiment-class predictions in this writeup).
not_entangled_with_eclipse_frozen_pred: EXP-283 (frozen prediction for Aug 2026 eclipse) and this EXP-285 are kept narratively separate. The Q3 implication paragraph mentions PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3 as one of two remaining live discriminators, but does not import any of EXP-283's three-bucket framing. EXP-283 is forward-looking; this writeup is backward-looking. The two narratives meet only at the level of 'what's left in the prospective bucket', not at the level of methodology.
newsworthy: First tacit-withdrawal verdict + zero-of-eleven monthly closure cohort — the cleanest snapshot to date of what the dome's prospective-bucket framework actually produces when it runs out a month of predictions on its own scheduled timeline.
